tweetText: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and its science team. This shift shows OpenAI's focus on enterprise AI over consumer projects. via TechCrunch AI

The gap between AI experts and the general public is growing as companies like OpenAI make big purchases and create new terms. This is important because it highlights rising concerns and confusion about AI's impact on society. via TechCrunch AI

Anthropic has launched Claude Design, a tool aimed at helping non-designers like founders quickly create visuals. This matters because it democratizes design, making it easier for everyone to express their ideas. via TechCrunch AI

The divide between AI experts and the general public is growing, highlighted by rising spending and new jargon. This matters because it raises concerns about accessibility and transparency in AI development. via TechCrunch AI

OpenAI executive Kevin Weil is leaving the company as his AI science application is being merged into Codex. This shift highlights ongoing changes in leadership and project focus at OpenAI. via WIRED AI
AI writing tools are starting to invade newsrooms, claiming to boost efficiency. This could seriously impact the quality and authenticity of journalism. via WIRED AI

OpenAI's Sora team leader, Bill Peebles, is leaving after the company scrapped its video generation tool. This shift reflects OpenAI's focus on coding and enterprise solutions, marking a significant change in its strategy. via The Verge AI

Tinder users can earn five free boosts by verifying their identity at a facial scanning orb co-founded by Sam Altman. This move highlights the growing trend of using tech to combat online impersonation and enhance user safety. via The Verge AI.

Anthropic's new cybersecurity model, Claude Mythos Preview, may improve its strained relationship with the Trump administration. This shift is crucial as it could help the company regain trust and access to government contracts. via The Verge AI

A new gadget called the Poetry Camera generates AI poetry instead of photos, leaving users charmed but frustrated. This quirky device raises questions about the value of creativity in tech and what we want from our gadgets. via The Verge AI.
